name: Forest
note: die-number TODO from physical cards; starts with 1 stored (gold field starts 0)

name: Hills
note: die-number TODO from physical cards; starts with 1 stored (gold field starts 0)

name: Pasture
note: die-number TODO from physical cards; starts with 1 stored (gold field starts 0)

name: Fields
note: die-number TODO from physical cards; starts with 1 stored (gold field starts 0)

name: Mountains
note: die-number TODO from physical cards; starts with 1 stored (gold field starts 0)

name: Gold Field
note: die-number TODO from physical cards; starts with 1 stored (gold field starts 0)

name: Settlement
rules_text: Worth 1 victory point. Provides 2 building sites (above and below). Can be upgraded to a city.
unclear: cost is the canonical Catan/Rivals build cost — confirm against the physical card

name: City
rules_text: Upgrade of a settlement. Worth 2 victory points.
unclear: cost is the canonical Catan/Rivals city-upgrade cost — confirm against the physical card

name: Road
rules_text: Connects settlements and lets you build an additional settlement. That is its only function.
unclear: cost is the canonical Catan/Rivals build cost — confirm against the physical card

name: Toll Bridge
rules_text: Event Plentiful Harvest: You receive 2 gold.

name: Storehouse
rules_text: Do not count the resources on the 2 neighboring regions when the event Brigand Attack is rolled.

name: Iron Foundry
rules_text: Doubles the ore production of the neighboring mountains.

name: Grain Mill
rules_text: Doubles the grain production of the neighboring fields.

name: Lumber Camp
rules_text: Doubles the lumber production of the neighboring forests.

name: Brick Factory
rules_text: Doubles the brick production of the neighboring hills.

name: Weaver's Shop
rules_text: Doubles the wool production of the neighboring pastures.

name: ABBEY
rules_text: Provides 1 progress point (a prerequisite for the University).
flavor_text: Progress is not the only thing here; you also get red wine and lots of dark beer.
unclear: No effect text transcribed — confirm against the physical card whether it has printed rules or is a prerequisite-only card

name: MARKETPLACE
rules_text: If a production number is rolled that appears more frequently on your opponent's regions than on yours, you receive 1 resource. Choose a resource your opponent can normally receive.

name: PARISH HALL
rules_text: You pay only 1 resource for choosing a card from a draw stack.

name: LARGE TRADE SHIP
rules_text: You may trade 2 resources of the left or right neighboring region for any 1 other resource of your choice.

name: GOLD SHIP
rules_text: During your turn, you may trade 2 gold for any 1 other resource as often as you wish.

name: ORE SHIP
rules_text: During your turn, you may trade 2 ore for any 1 other resource as often as you wish.

name: Grain Ship
rules_text: During your turn, you may trade 2 grain for any 1 other resource as often as you wish.

name: Lumber Ship
rules_text: During your turn, you may trade 2 lumber for any 1 other resource as often as you wish.

name: Brick Ship
rules_text: During your turn, you may trade 2 brick for any 1 other resource as often as you wish.

name: Wool Ship
rules_text: During your turn, you may trade 2 wool for any 1 other resource as often as you wish.

name: Austin
flavor_text: If you hit my left cheek, don't even think you'll have time to hit the right one too.

name: Harald
flavor_text: I knock you out faster than you can carve the word "strategy" in this stone.

name: Inga
flavor_text: The gods are expecting more offerings. And they expect you to hand them over to me!

name: Osmund
flavor_text: When it comes to gold and women, friendship stops. And it also stops when it comes to power. Actually, friendship never lasts very long.

name: Candamir
flavor_text: A well-sharpened axe is a tried and tested starting position for a successful conversation.

name: Siglind
flavor_text: Turning men into heroes belittles the importance of being a hero.

name: Brigitta, the Wise Woman
rules_text: Play this card before rolling the dice. Choose the result of the production die roll.

name: Relocation
rules_text: You may exchange 2 of your own regions or 2 of your own expansion cards. Resources stored on regions may not be changed and card placement rules must be followed.

name: Scout
rules_text: Play this card when building a settlement. Take 2 cards of your choice from the region card stack. Reshuffle the region card stack.

name: Merchant Caravan
rules_text: Discard exactly 2 of your resources and take any 2 resources of your choice in return.

name: Goldsmith
rules_text: Discard 3 gold and take any 2 resources of your choice in return.

name: Invention
rules_text: Each player gets any 1 resource of his choice for each building with a progress point – up to a maximum of 2 resources.

name: Yule
rules_text: Shuffle the event card stack as performed at the beginning of the game. Afterwards, draw an event card again.

name: Year of Plenty
rules_text: Each region gets 1 resource for each adjacent Storehouse and Abbey, provided that storage space is available.

name: Fraternal Feuds
rules_text: The player who has the strength advantage selects 2 cards from the opponent's hand and returns them to the bottom of matching draw stacks.

name: Feud
rules_text: The player who has the strength advantage selects 3 of his opponent's buildings. The opponent must remove one of them and return it to the bottom of a matching draw stack.

name: Traveling Merchant
rules_text: Each player may take up to 2 resources of his choice, paying 1 gold per resource.

name: TRADE SHIPS RACE
rules_text: The player who owns the most trade ships receives any 1 resource of his choice. In case of a tie, each player receives any 1 resource of his choice (each must have at least 1 trade ship).

name: STOREHOUSE
rules_text: Do not count the resources on the 2 neighboring regions when the event Brigand Attack is rolled.

name: TOLL BRIDGE
rules_text: Event Plentiful Harvest: You receive 2 gold.

name: GOLD CACHE

values.requires: Hero with at least 1 strength point

rules_text: The Gold Cache may also be used to store the gold you received. When the event Brigand Attack is rolled, the gold in the cache is neither counted nor stolen. Requires: Hero with at least 1 strength point.

name: PIRATE SHIP
rules_text: Your opponent must remove 1 trade ship of his choice from his principality and place it on the discard pile. Event Plentiful Harvest: You receive 1 gold.

name: LARGE TRADE SHIP
rules_text: You may trade 2 resources of the left or right neighboring region for any 1 other resource of your choice.

name: MERCHANT GUILD
flavor_text: Money can't buy you happiness. But taking it away from others can.

name: MONEYLENDER
rules_text: If you have the trade advantage and the event Trade is rolled on the event die, you may take 2 resources of your choice from your opponent.

name: HARBOR

values.note: worth +1 VP while >=3 trade ships in play

rules_text: As long as at least 3 trade ships are placed in your principality, the Harbor is worth 1 victory point.

name: TRADING BASE
rules_text: The Marketplace and the Harbor receive a second commerce point.

name: MINT
rules_text: Once per each of your turns, you may use this Mint to trade 1 gold for 1 other resource of your choice.

name: STAPLE HOUSE
rules_text: If you build the Staple House, you immediately receive 2 resources of your choice. Requires: Merchant Guild.

name: Salt Silo
rules_text: Each of your trade ships is worth 1 more commerce point.

name: Trade Master
rules_text: You immediately receive 2 resources of your choice from your opponent. Requires: Merchant Guild.

name: Merchant
rules_text: Take up to 2 resources of your choice from your opponent and give him 1 resource of your choice in return. Requires: 3 commerce points or city.

name: Reiner the Herald
rules_text: Play this card before rolling the dice and determine the event Celebration. You receive 1 additional resource for the Celebration.

name: Goldsmith
rules_text: Discard 3 gold and take any 2 resources of your choice in return.

name: Brigands
rules_text: You may take as many resources of the same type from your opponent as 1 of your regions can accommodate. Requires: Strength advantage.

name: Gudrun, Terror of the Seas
rules_text: For each of your Pirate Ships, your opponent must give you up to 2 gold.

name: Traveling Merchant
rules_text: Each player may take up to 2 resources of his choice. For each resource, 1 gold must be paid.

name: Trade Ships Race
rules_text: The player who owns the most trade ships receives any 1 resource of his choice. In case of a tie, each player receives any 1 resource of his choice (each must have at least 1 trade ship).

name: Gift for the Prince
rules_text: Each player receives 1 gold for each unit with at least 1 strength point.

name: Drill Ground
rules_text: Each hero you build in your principality costs you 1 resource of your choice less.

name: Lookout Tower
rules_text: When your opponent plays an Archer, Arsonist, or Traitor, roll the die. If you roll a 1 or 2, the card has no effect.

name: Carl Forkbeard
flavor_text: What a beautiful island! I take it.

name: Heinrich the Sentinel
rules_text: When your opponent plays an Archer, Arsonist, or Traitor, roll the die. If you roll a 3, 4, or 5, the card has no effect.

name: Irmgard, Keeper of the Light
rules_text: If you lose a card of your principality due to an event or an action, you receive any 1 resource of your choice.

name: Chapel
rules_text: If a 1, 2, or 3 is rolled with the production die, the event Riots does not apply to you.

name: Fairgrounds
rules_text: If you have more skill points than your opponent, you immediately receive 2 resources of your choice after building the Fairground.

name: Hedge Tavern (1x)
rules_text: Required to play Archer, Arsonist, and Traitor.
flavor_text: In the neighborhood, word has it that more people were seen going inside than coming out.
unclear: No effect text transcribed — confirm against the physical card whether it has printed rules or is a prerequisite-only card

name: Tithe Barn
rules_text: After building the Tithe Barn, you immediately receive either grain or wool – that is, one of the chosen resource for each of your heroes.

name: Large Festival Hall
rules_text: Worth 2 victory points.
flavor_text: Just a moment! Didn't the estimate specify a much bigger tower?
unclear: values: two red banner/flag icons at bottom-right – unsure what point value (e.g. victory_points or strength) they represent
cost: top icon row read as 2 gold, 2 ore, 2 brick but icon counts/types should be verified against physical card

name: Fire Brigade
rules_text: This city's buildings are safe from the Arsonist.

name: Voyage of Plunder
rules_text: If your opponent has more victory points, he must give you 2 resources of your choice. If he isn't in the lead, you only receive one resource. Requires: Strength advantage.

name: Archer
rules_text: Your opponent must place 1 of his own units with at least 1 strength point under a matching draw stack. Requires: Hedge Tavern.

name: Arsonist
rules_text: Choose 1 of your opponent's buildings adjacent to a settlement/city. He must place it under a draw stack of his choice. Requires: Hedge Tavern.

name: Brigands
rules_text: You may take as many resources of the same type from your opponent as 1 of your regions can accommodate. Requires: Strength advantage.

name: Sebastian the Itinerant Preacher
rules_text: If you play this card when the events Riots, Feud, or Fraternal Feud occur, these events do not apply to you.

name: Traitor
rules_text: Your opponent must show you all the cards in his hand. You may add 1 of them to your hand (in the Tournament Game, only units and action cards). Requires: Hedge Tavern.

name: Riots
rules_text: A player who has 1 or 2 units with strength points or commerce points pays 1 gold. A player who has more than 2 of these units pays 2 gold. If a player doesn't pay, he must remove one of these units and return it to the bottom of a matching draw stack.

name: Fraternal Feuds
rules_text: The player who has the strength advantage selects 2 cards from the opponent's hand and returns them to the bottom of matching draw stacks.

name: Feud
rules_text: The player who has the strength advantage selects 3 of his opponent's buildings. The opponent must remove one of them and return it to the bottom of a matching draw stack.

name: Chief Cannoneer
rules_text: Requires: University.
flavor_text: I'll show you how to produce a romantic castle ruin.

name: TOWN HALL
rules_text: Place the Town Hall on your Parish Hall. At the end of your turn, you no longer pay for choosing a card.

name: UNIVERSITY
rules_text: Requires: Abbey or Library.
flavor_text: Formerly, there was a hedge-tavern here. Now there are two of them next door.

name: LIBRARY
rules_text: When you build the Library, you may immediately choose a card from a draw stack (Tournament: from your own stack).

name: PHARMACY
rules_text: When the event Plague occurs, you receive any 1 resource of your choice. You receive this resource whether you previously lost resources or not.

name: BATH HOUSE
rules_text: Protects all 4 regions bordering this city from the effects of the event Plague.

name: BUILDING CRANE
rules_text: Every city expansion you build that costs more than 4 resources costs you 1 resource less. Requires: University.

name: PARLIAMENT

values.other: two region-card/road symbols shown at right of title (orange settlement icons)

rules_text: For the benefit of the people and their representatives in particular. Requires: 2 progress points.
flavor_text: For the benefit of the people and their representatives in particular.

name: THREE-FIELD SYSTEM

values.other: A (action card letter, top-left in shield)

rules_text: You receive up to 2 grain. Requires: University.

name: MINERAL MINING

values.other: A (action card letter, top-left in shield)

rules_text: You receive up to 2 ore. Requires: University.

name: Benjamin the Traveling Scholar
rules_text: You once more receive the resource of each region whose number you rolled at the beginning of your turn.

name: Guido the Ambassador
rules_text: You may choose 1 card from the discard pile (Tournament: from your opponent's discard pile). Requires: Town Hall or fewer victory points than your opponent.

name: Gustav the Librarian
rules_text: You may choose 1 card from the discard pile (Tournament: from your own discard pile). Requires: Library or fewer victory points than your opponent.

name: Doctor
rules_text: Each region bordering your Bath House receives 1 resource. If you have various Bath Houses, you may only use 1 of them.

name: Brigitta, the Wise Woman
rules_text: Play this card before rolling the dice. Choose the result of the production die roll.

name: Relocation
rules_text: You may exchange 2 of your own regions or 2 of your own expansion cards. Resources stored on regions may not be changed and card placement rules must be followed.

name: Plague
rules_text: Every region bordering a city loses 1 resource.

name: INVENTION
rules_text: Each player gets any 1 resource of his choice for each building with a progress point – up to a maximum of 2 resources.

name: Pilgrimage Site
rules_text: If an event or an opponent's action makes you lose cards from your hand, immediately draw back up to your hand limit.

name: Great Thingstead
rules_text: Neither player may play action-attack cards, and the event Religious Dispute no longer affects either player. Requires: Church and Odin's Temple.

name: Odin's Fountain
rules_text: At the end of your turn you may exchange 2 cards instead of 1.

name: Reiner the Miller
rules_text: Place on a fields region. Trade that region's grain 2:1 for any resource; once per turn 1:1 if it borders a Grain Mill.

name: Abbey Brewery
rules_text: Place on a fields region. Pay 2 grain to rotate it to any side of your principality. Requires: City.

name: Red Light Tavern
rules_text: Foreign card built on the opponent's free road. The opponent's units count as only 1 strength. Removed automatically once the opponent has 3 heroines.

name: Judith, Guardian of the Church
rules_text: Place on your Church to protect it from actions, expansion effects and events. Once per turn, pay 1 non-gold resource to receive up to 2 gold. Judith + Church are worth 2 VP together.

name: Bran, Defender of the Temple
rules_text: Place on your Odin's Temple. Draw 2 cards when built. Protects the Temple. Bran + Temple are worth 2 VP together.

name: Master of the Brotherhood
rules_text: Caps how much gold an opponent's action card can cost you, and you receive up to 1 gold. Requires: not owning or building an Abbey.

name: Godfrey the Intriguer
rules_text: On a later turn, pay 1 gold to view the opponent's hand and take 1 card; if you take one, discard Godfrey afterwards.

name: Church
rules_text: When built, immediately receive up to 2 gold. Prerequisite for several Intrigue cards.

name: Odin's Temple
rules_text: When built, immediately draw 1 card from any draw stack. Prerequisite for several Intrigue cards.

name: Sacrificial Site
rules_text: Trade wool 2:1 for any resource. Wool on adjacent pastures is safe from the Brigand. Requires: Odin's Fountain or Odin's Temple.

name: Bishop's See
rules_text: When built, choose 3 of the opponent's units; they remove 1 of them. Requires: Church, Abbey or Chapel.

name: Missionary
rules_text: The opponent removes 1 of their heroes; place it in your principality or discard it. Requires: Church or Bishop's See.

name: Bishop
rules_text: Demand 1 gold from the opponent, then receive as much gold as one gold field can hold. Requires: Church or Bishop's See.

name: Odin's Priest
rules_text: The opponent reveals their hand and places all action cards and units under matching draw stacks. Requires: Odin's Temple.

name: Priestess of the Norns
rules_text: Choose a draw stack and take up to 2 cards from it. Requires: Odin's Fountain or Odin's Temple.

name: Michael the Master Builder
rules_text: When building, you may replace 1-3 of the required resources with 1 gold each.

name: Good Neighbors
rules_text: A peaceful event - no attack occurs. (See the card for its effect on face-up cards left on the draw stacks.)

name: Religious Dispute
rules_text: Each player with at least 1 city loses cards from their hand (placed under matching draw stacks). A Church and an Odin's Temple each reduce how many you lose.

name: Wainwright
rules_text: Once per turn, move resources between two of your regions of the same type, or trade 3:1.

name: Cloth Merchant's Residence
rules_text: Place adjacent to a pasture region. For 2 wool from the adjacent pasture region, you may rotate the Residence to the next higher level. Requires: City.

name: Paper Merchant's Residence
rules_text: Place adjacent to a forest region. For 2 lumber from the adjacent forest region, you may rotate the Residence to the next higher level. Requires: City.

name: Trading Post
rules_text: Road complement on your own free road. Once per turn, convert 1 resource between its two adjacent regions 1:1.

name: Brigand Camp
rules_text: Foreign card built on the opponent's free road. The opponent's commerce is reduced by 1, and whenever they store a resource gained via their Marketplace you receive 1 gold.

name: Ship Builder
rules_text: Each of your trade ships costs 1 lumber or 1 wool less. Once, pay 1 gold to retrieve a trade ship from the discard pile.

name: Olaf the Merchant Ship Captain
rules_text: On a later turn, demand 1-2 resources and give 1 back, then discard Olaf.

name: Pirate Ship
rules_text: When built, the opponent removes 1 trade ship to the discard. On Plentiful Harvest, gain 1 gold.

name: Commercial Metropolis
rules_text: Place on one of your cities. Worth 2 VP (4 total with the city). Cannot be removed or attacked. Requires: a top-level Residence or 6 commerce points.

name: Trading Station
rules_text: Foreign card built on the opponent's city. Once per turn, pay 1 gold to buy 1 resource from them. Requires: Commercial Harbor.

name: Commercial Harbor
rules_text: Once per turn, downgrade one of your Residences by 1 level to take any 2 resources. Prerequisite for several cards.

name: Lighthouse
rules_text: Once per turn, use a trade ship adjacent to it to trade 1:1. Immune to Capricious Sea. Requires: Commercial Harbor or 2+ trade ships.

name: Craft Guild
rules_text: When built, rotate all your Residences up 1 level (or take 2 resources if you have none). Prerequisite.

name: Master Merchants' Alliance
rules_text: When the opponent makes a 2:1 or 3:1 trade in their principality, you receive 1 of the resource they paid.

name: Guild Master
rules_text: Take up to 2 resources of your choice. Requires: Craft Guild.

name: Hergild the Master Merchant
rules_text: This turn, freely trade one trade-ship resource type 1:1. Requires: Commercial Harbor or 2+ trade ships.

name: Gero the Master Merchant
rules_text: Take any 1-2 resources of your choice. Requires: 2+ trade ships.

name: Tactical Retreat
rules_text: Remove one of your buildings from the opponent's principality to gain any 2 resources (1 of them taken from the opponent).

name: Mendicants
rules_text: If the opponent has the trade advantage, demand 1 resource (2 if they also lead on VP).

name: Trade Monopoly
rules_text: Demand 1-3 resources of a single type; give 1 back. Requires: Commercial Harbor.

name: Maritime Trade Monopoly
rules_text: Demand 1 resource for each trade ship you have more than the opponent (max 2).

name: Fortunate Trade Voyage
rules_text: A favourable voyage - each player receives resources according to their trade ships.

name: Capricious Sea
rules_text: Calm Sea (1, 2, 3, 4): each player receives any 1 resource of their choice for each of their own trade ships. Storm (5, 6): each player slides one of their trade ships under a matching draw stack.

name: Hour of the Master Merchants
rules_text: Rotate each of your Residences to the next higher level. For each Residence already at the highest level, receive 1 resource in its adjacent region.

name: White Raven Tavern
rules_text: 1x during your turn: Pay 1 gold and roll the dice. On 1-2: gain nothing. On 3-5: gain 1 resource. On 6: gain 2 resources.

name: Border Fortress
rules_text: Place adjacent to a hills region where at least 1 brick is stored. Pay this brick. For 1 ore + 1 wool, rotate the Border Fortress to the next level.

name: Triumph Card
rules_text: Place adjacent to any region of your choice. If you win a Barbarian Attack, instead of taking 2 resources, rotate the Triumph Card to the next level. Requires: City.

name: Barbarian Stronghold
rules_text: Place on a free road adjacent to one of your opponent's cities. For your opponent, the barbarian strength increases by 1. Removal: Opponent's Triumph Card shows 3 victory points.

name: Arnd the Strategist
rules_text: When a Barbarian Attack occurs, each hero in the settlement/city where Arnd is placed counts as 2 units—except for Arnd himself.

name: Baroc the Barbarian
rules_text: After a Brigand Attack, gain 1 ore. If there is a Barbarian Stronghold in your opponent's principality, gain 2 ore instead.

name: Siward the Scout
rules_text: 1x during your turn: View the top 3 cards of the event stack or any draw stack for free, or pay 1 gold to view your opponent's hand.

name: Wolfgang the Street Performer
rules_text: Celebration: Add 1 resource to each region adjacent to Wolfgang. If you gain resources, your opponent may place Wolfgang in their principality by paying 2 gold.

name: Caravel
rules_text: Each ship in the settlement/city where the Caravel is placed counts double in case of a Barbarian Attack—except for the Caravel itself. Requires: City.

name: Marie the Shieldmaiden
rules_text: During one of your following turns, demand that your opponent choose 2 heroes that can be placed on Marie's building site. Place 1 of them on Marie's site. Place Marie on the discard pile.

name: Secret Brotherhood
rules_text: (1) If you lose against the barbarians, pay only 1 resource. (2) 1x during your turn: If you discard an unplayed unit or action card, gain any 1 resource of your choice.

name: Bailwick
rules_text: 1x during your turn: Draw the top card from a draw stack (from your own draw stack).

name: Castle
rules_text: For calculating your strength in a Barbarian Attack, each strength point of your Border Fortress counts as 1 additional unit.

name: Arsenal
rules_text: In case of a Brigand Attack, resources in regions adjacent to the Arsenal are neither counted nor stolen.

name: Siegfried, Vanquisher of the Barbarians
rules_text: Take 1 or 2 resources of your choice or rotate your Triumph Card to the next level. Requires: Castle and at least 2 heroes.

name: Alliance Against the Barbarians

values.requires: Triumph Card indicating at least 1 victory point, plus at least 1 unit.

rules_text: Each player with at least 1 unit takes any 1 resource of choice. The player with the most units takes up to 1 additional resource. Requires: Triumph Card (≥1 victory point) + ≥1 unit.

name: Castellan
rules_text: The two regions adjacent to your Castle each receive 1 resource—if storage space is available. Requires: Castle.

name: Relocation
rules_text: Swap 2 of your own regions or 2 of your own expansion cards. Resource count and card placement must remain unchanged and compliant with rules.

name: Contest of the Heroes
rules_text: Name 1 of your heroes and 1 opponent hero (if they have one). Each rolls the die and adds strength points. Tie: roll again. Winner gains 1 resource and may take 1 more from loser.

name: Barbarian Attack
rules_text: Player with fewer units than city/metropolis/expansion victory points discards 2 resources. Player with more units + ≥1 city gains 2 resources. Card then goes under top 4 cards of event stack.

name: Retreat of the Barbarians
rules_text: Player with ≥1 unit chooses 1 card from 1 draw stack. Player with ≥1 unit + strength advantage chooses up to 2 cards from 1 draw stack.

name: Shipwreck
rules_text: Discovery: You receive any 1 resource of your choice. Missions to the Shipwreck are not possible.

name: Island of the Bards
rules_text: Discovery: Your opponent shows you their hand of cards; you may draw 1 action card or unit. Mission: Pay 2 grain to rotate to next higher level.

name: Island of the Merchants
rules_text: Discovery: Trade any 1 resource of your choice for any 2 other resources of your choice. Mission: Pay 2 wool to rotate to next higher level.

name: Islands of the Scholars
rules_text: Discovery: Choose up to 2 cards from any 1 draw stack of your choice and draw them. Mission: Pay 2 lumber to rotate to next higher level.

name: Haidao Chang
rules_text: Discovery/Mission: Pay 1 gold or fight. Fight: Roll production die and add cannons. Victory: Rotate to next higher level and take bounty. Defeat: Lose ship. Tie: Roll again.

name: Cimmarone
rules_text: Discovery/Mission: Pay 1 gold or fight. Fight: Roll production die and add cannons. Victory: Rotate to next higher level and take bounty. Defeat: Lose ship. Tie: Roll again.

name: Jean
rules_text: Discovery/Mission: Pay 2 resources or fight. Fight: Roll production die and add cannons. Victory: Rotate to next higher level and take bounty. Defeat: Lose ship. Tie: Roll again.

name: Island of the Forgotten Tribe
rules_text: Discovery: Return ship to Explorer Harbor; carry out 1 additional mission. Mission: Roll production die—1: Reef (lose ship); 2-4: Event (roll event die); 5-6: Treasure (rotate to next higher level).

name: Explorer Harbor
rules_text: Build explorer ships (1 lumber + 1 wool; stand ship upright). You may make only 1 discovery per turn.

name: Explorer Metropolis

values.requires: Place on the city adjacent to the Explorer Harbor. Requires: At least 6 discovered sea cards or at least 2 level-3 islands.

rules_text: Place on city adjacent to Explorer Harbor. Worth 4 victory points total. Requires: 6+ discovered sea cards or 2+ level 3 islands.

name: Armory
rules_text: Gives 1 strength point and 1 cannon point.

name: Sailmakers' Shop
rules_text: Gives 1 commerce point and 1 sail point.

name: Landing Stage
rules_text: 1x per turn on mission to Island of Bards, Scholars, or Merchants: Replace 1 required resource with 1 gold.

name: Shipyard
rules_text: 1x per turn: Trade any 1 resource for 1 lumber or 1 wool.

name: Astronomer
rules_text: 1x per turn: If you have fewer sea cards discovered than opponent, pay 1 gold for second journey of exploration.

name: Cannon Foundry
rules_text: 1x per turn: If you lose against pirate, repeat die roll once.

name: Cartographer
rules_text: View up to 2 face-down sea cards and/or switch positions of any 2 sea cards in your principality. Requires: At least 1 sail point.

name: Broadside
rules_text: Opponent rotates 1 island card to next lower level or loses 1 explorer ship—you choose. Requires: At least 2 cannon points.

name: Lars the Naval Hero
rules_text: When fighting pirate, die roll is omitted and pirate is defeated. Requires: At least 1 cannon point.

name: Navigator
rules_text: Return 1 used explorer ship to Explorer Harbor. Perform another action with this ship. Requires: 2 sail points.

name: Ambassador

values.requires: You have rotated the respective card at least to level 1.

rules_text: Gain advantage of discovering Island of Bards, Island of Scholars, or Island of Merchants another time. Requires: Card rotated to level 1 or higher.

name: Friendship Between Peoples
rules_text: Each player receives any 1 resource of his choice for each Island card he has rotated at least to the next higher level.

name: Most Successful Explorer
rules_text: The player who discovered the most sea cards (at least 1) may draw up to 2 cards from any 1 draw stack of their choice (from their own draw stacks). Tie: each player may draw 1 card, the player whose turn it is going first.

name: Grove of Freedom
rules_text: 1x during your turn: Choose 1 card from a draw stack of your choice without changing the order. You may immediately use it; place excess under a matching stack at turn end.

name: Grove of Fraternity
rules_text: 1x during your turn: Draw up to 3 cards from any of your draw stacks. You may immediately use them; excess cards go under matching stacks at turn end. Opponent then draws 1 card from their choice of draw stacks.

name: Grove of Justice
rules_text: Before your die roll: When your opponent plays an action attack card, roll the production die. If result > 3, the card has no effect and is discarded. (Works with Brigitta without rolling.)

name: Grove of Peace
rules_text: When your opponent plays an action attack card: Roll the production die. If result > 3, the card has no effect and your opponent must discard it. (If Robert Herald is placed, result of 3 also blocks it.)

name: Grove of Vigilance
rules_text: When Trade, Celebration, or Brigand Attack is rolled: Declare the event void; instead Event Card applies. (For Brigand Attack, each player receives resources first, then draw event card.)

name: Grove of Great Foresight
rules_text: 1x during your turn: View the 3 topmost event cards. Reorder some under the stack as desired without altering the top card's position or including Yule.

name: Grove of Courage
rules_text: You may use this grove during your opponent's turn. When you would lose cards from hand or buildings to a Feud or Fraternal Feud event, those events have no effect.

name: Manifesto of Humane Conduct
rules_text: Rotate to next level: Pay 1 lumber, 2 gold, 3 owls. For 2 of those owls, you may place 1 action attack card on the discard pile. Requires: City.

name: Peter, Sage of the Forest
rules_text: Place adjacent to a forest region with at least 1 lumber. Pay this lumber. When the region's number is rolled during production, rotate Peter to the next higher level.

name: Michaela, Sage of the Pasture
rules_text: Place adjacent to a pasture region with at least 1 wool. Pay this wool. When the region's number is rolled during production, rotate Michaela to the next higher level.

name: Barbara, Sage of the Fields
rules_text: Place adjacent to a fields region with at least 1 grain. Pay this grain. When the region's number is rolled during production, rotate Barbara to the next higher level.

name: Frederich, Sage of the Hills
rules_text: Place adjacent to a hills region with at least 1 brick. Pay this brick. When the region's number is rolled during production, rotate Frederich to the next higher level.

name: Piet, Sage of the Mountains
rules_text: Place adjacent to a mountains region with at least 1 ore. Pay this ore. When the region's number is rolled during production, rotate Piet to the next higher level.

name: Walther, Sage of the Gold Field
rules_text: Place adjacent to a gold field region with at least 1 gold. Pay this gold. When the region's number is rolled during production, rotate Walther to the next higher level. Feud/Fraternal Feud events have no effect on you.

name: Principal Sage Woman
rules_text: Place adjacent to any region with at least 1 resource. Pay this resource. When the region's number is rolled during production, rotate the Principal Sage Woman to the next higher level.

name: Robert, Herald of the Sages
rules_text: When you must roll the production die to surpass a card value or your opponent's roll, you win also on a tie.

name: Cole, Paladin of the Sages
rules_text: 1x during your turn: Use the function of one of your opponent's groves by paying the number of owls indicated on it.

name: Granary
rules_text: 1x during your turn: If your opponent's fields region has no grain, give them 1 grain (which they must store there). Then demand any 1 resource of a different type from them. (You can only build 1 per principality.)

name: Academy of Sages
rules_text: 1x during your turn: Pay any 1 resource of your choice and rotate any 1 sage card to the next higher level. (If both copies are built, you may provide 2 sages 1 owl each per turn instead.)

name: Courthouse
rules_text: When the Trade event is rolled: You determine which resources you give to your opponent. (You can only build 1 per principality.)

name: Great Foresight
rules_text: Pay 1 owl: View the event card stack and remove—without changing card order—1 event card except Yule.

name: Dispute of the Sages

values.requires: Both players each have at least 1 sage.

rules_text: Pay 1 owl: You and opponent roll the production die; each adds their sages' owls to their result. Winner receives any 2 resources of their choice. Tie: Both receive 1 resource. Requires: Both players have ≥1 sage.

name: Wise Compensation

values.requires: Fewer cities or victory points than your opponent.

rules_text: Pay 3 owls: Build 1 city for free without paying resources. Requires: Fewer cities or victory points than your opponent.

name: Power of the Groves
rules_text: Pay 1 owl: Receive 1 owl for each grove in your principality. Distribute these owls among your sages as desired. Requires: At least 2 sages.

name: Wise Protection
rules_text: Pay 1 owl: Your opponent must show you their hand. If it contains an action attack card, they must place all hand cards under matching draw stacks of their own.

name: Famine
rules_text: Each player without a Granary must discard 1 grain. If a player has no grain, they must discard 2 resources of their choice.

name: Council of the Sages
rules_text: Each player may distribute at their own discretion either up to 2 owls among their sages OR up to 2 resources among regions with an adjacent sage.

name: Common Land
rules_text: 1x during your turn: Pay 1 star to trade any 1 resource of your choice for 1 different resource.

name: Village School
rules_text: After building, you immediately receive up to 2 stars. You receive 1 additional star each time the event Trade occurs.

name: Traveling Theater
rules_text: Place on a free road. You receive 1 star. 1x during your turn: Move to another free road. Trade 1 adjacent region's resource for 1 star.

name: Mercenaries
rules_text: Place on a free road. You may keep 1 gold and 1 wool during Brigand Attack; pay 1 star to keep all resources.

name: Small Market Town
rules_text: Place on a free road. 1x during your turn: If an adjacent region has 3 resources, trade 1 of them for any 1 different resource.

name: Public Feeling
rules_text: Pay 1 star to prevent Riots and Insurrection from affecting you. 1x during your turn: For 2 stars, receive any 1 resource of your choice.

name: Thieves' Hideout
rules_text: Place adjacent to an opponent's free region. 1x during your turn: For 1 wool, move to another opponent's free region. Removed if opponent has 5+ strength or pays 3 stars.

name: Prince
rules_text: 1x during your turn: For 1 gold, retrieve 1 Artwork from the discard pile. Cannot be taken or enticed away. Requires: Not having a Princess.

name: Princess
rules_text: 1x during your turn: For 1 gold, retrieve 1 Artwork from the discard pile. Cannot be taken or enticed away. Requires: Not having a Prince.

name: Aqueduct
rules_text: If a field or pasture region number is rolled, you receive 1 additional resource from that region. Can only build 1 Aqueduct.

name: Hospital
rules_text: 1x during your turn: Receive 1 star, or pay 1 star and receive any 1 resource of your choice. Requires: Aqueduct.

name: Theater
rules_text: After building, you immediately receive 1 star.

name: Monument to the Prince
rules_text: After building, your opponent determines 2 of their placed units. Choose 1 of them and add it to your hand.

name: City Palace
rules_text: 1x during your turn: Pay 3 stars and draw 1 action card with no requirement, or 1 unit from the discard pile into your hand.

name: Builder's Hut
rules_text: (1) If event Celebration is rolled, receive 1 star. (2) A building costing 6+ resources costs 1 resource less. Can only build 1 Builders' Hut.

name: Feeding the Poor
rules_text: Pay 1 grain. You receive up to 2 stars.

name: Artwork: Sculpture
rules_text: You receive 1 star. If you have a Builders' Hut, receive another 1 star. Requires: Prince or Princess.

name: Artwork: Epic
rules_text: You receive 1 star. If you have a Theater, receive another 1 star. Requires: Prince or Princess.

name: Artwork: Fountain
rules_text: You receive 1 star. If you have an Aqueduct, receive another 1 star. Requires: Prince or Princess.

name: Artwork: Relief
rules_text: You receive 1 star. If you have a City Palace, receive another 1 star. Requires: Prince or Princess.

name: Court Astrologer
rules_text: Pay 1 star and you may once more determine an event die roll result during your turn. Requires: Prince or Princess.

name: Bera the Insurrectionist
rules_text: You choose: Your opponent gives you up to 2 stars, or 1 resource of their choice for each VP they have in excess of yours (max 3 resources). Requires: Public Feeling.

name: Prosperity
rules_text: Building a city costs you only 1 ore and 2 grain. Requires: Aqueduct.

name: Insurrection
rules_text: Each player must place 1 building costing at least 2 resources under a matching draw stack. Then place this card under the 4 topmost event card stack cards.

name: Taxation
rules_text: Each player who pays 1 star receives any 1 resource of their choice and 1 gold.
